title: Shotokan Ohshima Karate-do & Self-Defense
date_time: Tuesday, March 3 2026 at 5:45 PM CST to Tuesday, March 3 2026 at 7:30 PM CST
location: Nebraska Union Ballroom 1400 R St, Lincoln, NE View Map
description: Please see our website and Google calendar for the most up-to-date practice schedule: https://unl.ska.org/karate-class-schedule/ Shotokan Ohshima is a very traditional style and differs from many martial arts schools. Comprehensive, challenging exercise is only one of the many benefits of karate practice. Teaching traditional karate-do as a serious art and way of life means much more. It ensures the original ideas of the founder are passed on to our students, so that we grow in all aspects of our lives. Altman Senpai pushes us to challenge ourselves and taste our life, to be more fully aware and conscientious at all times. Our karate practice can be very difficult. The main difficulty lies in training one's mind to be disciplined and focused. Remember, anyone can be lazy, because that is the easiest thing to do! Karate may be defined as a weapon-less means of self-defense. These techniques use all parts of the body to their maximum advantage. Our fundamental techniques are divided into three main aspects: - Kihon — Fundamental blocks, punches, kicks, and stances - Kata — Pre-arranged forms simulating combat situations - Kumite — Sparring In each category, the beginner is given instruction at the most basic level until the techniques become spontaneous to them. As the student progresses technically, the practice progresses physically and the student begins to build greater stamina. At this stage, one involves himself or herself with the more intricate and difficult kata and more dynamic forms of kumite. Karate as self-defense is one of the most dynamic of all the martial arts. The trained karate student is able to coordinate the mind and body perfectly, thereby allowing one to unleash tremendous physical power at will. It is not the possession of great physical strength that makes a strong karateka; rather it is the ability to coordinate the mind and body. Upon developing this ability, even the smallest person finds that within themselves is the power to deliver a devastating blow to any would-be attacker. The values of karate to modern life are numerous. In our everyday lives we often forget the value of exercise to both our physical and mental health. The practice of karate tones the body, develops coordination, quickens reflexes, and builds stamina. Also, the serious practice of karate develops composure, a clearer thought process, deeper insight into one's mental capabilities, and more self-confidence. In this, karate is not an end, but a means to an end. It is an activity in which advancing age is not a hindrance. Rather, it encourages proficiency and the keen coordination of mind and body.

title: College Republicans Weekly Meeting
date_time: Wednesday, March 4 2026 at 6:00 PM CST to Wednesday, March 4 2026 at 7:00 PM CST
description: Come hang out with the College Republicans! We are a registered group on campus dedicated to fostering and encouraging the activities of the Republican Party and promoting its ideals. We serve as an effective political voice through discussing and disseminating information on the local, state, national, and international levels. Our organization serves as a quasi-lobby in an effort to influence governmental affairs on a local, state, and national level. We encourage college students to express their political views through the philosophy of the Republican Party. Republicans believe in liberty, economic prosperity, preserving American values and traditions, and restoring the American dream for every citizen of this great nation. Through College Republicans, we wish to bring the next generation of Republicans into the party in order to grow and bring new ideas to the Republican Party through debate and discussion. We extend an invite to anyone on campus, regardless of their political orientation, background, or interest level to attend our weekly meetings to discuss the political issues going on in our local towns, cities, states, and nations, and have some fun!

title: Uplift Huskers
date_time: Saturday, March 7 2026 at 3:00 PM CST to Saturday, March 7 2026 at 7:00 PM CST
location: East campus union 1705 Arbor Drive Lincoln, NE 68503, Lincoln, NE View Map
description: Uplift Husker addresses international student isolation through culturally inclusive peer supportwhile welcoming all UNL students and community members. The event combines facilitated small-group reflections, collaborative board games, West African cultural activities including drummingand dance led by FACS members with African cultural heritage, culturally diverse cuisinerepresenting multiple global regions, singing, and physical movement activities to create asupportive space for cross-cultural connection and emotional sharing. Unlike generic campusevents, Uplift Husker intentionally centers activities that bridge cultural differences: West Africandrumming circles led by FACS members provide both stress relief and cultural exchange; diversecuisine creates conversation opportunities; and facilitated discussions address challenges specific tointernational students (homesickness, cultural adjustment, navigating unfamiliar academic systems)while building empathy among domestic students. This event serves as the pilot launch for HuskerWellbeing Circle, a sustained monthly peer network. Participants become founding members, usingcommunity identifier t-shirts to recognize each other across campus. Monthly meetups will continuefor two months following the main event, featuring board games, discussion circles, and light refreshments.

title: Shotokan Ohshima Karate-do & Self-Defense
date_time: Wednesday, March 11 2026 at 5:45 PM CDT to Wednesday, March 11 2026 at 7:30 PM CDT
location: Nebraska Union 1400 R St, Lincoln, NE View Map
description: Please see our website and Google calendar for the most up-to-date practice schedule: https://unl.ska.org/karate-class-schedule/ Shotokan Ohshima is a very traditional style and differs from many martial arts schools. Comprehensive, challenging exercise is only one of the many benefits of karate practice. Teaching traditional karate-do as a serious art and way of life means much more. It ensures the original ideas of the founder are passed on to our students, so that we grow in all aspects of our lives. Altman Senpai pushes us to challenge ourselves and taste our life, to be more fully aware and conscientious at all times. Our karate practice can be very difficult. The main difficulty lies in training one's mind to be disciplined and focused. Remember, anyone can be lazy, because that is the easiest thing to do! Karate may be defined as a weapon-less means of self-defense. These techniques use all parts of the body to their maximum advantage. Our fundamental techniques are divided into three main aspects: - Kihon — Fundamental blocks, punches, kicks, and stances - Kata — Pre-arranged forms simulating combat situations - Kumite — Sparring In each category, the beginner is given instruction at the most basic level until the techniques become spontaneous to them. As the student progresses technically, the practice progresses physically and the student begins to build greater stamina. At this stage, one involves himself or herself with the more intricate and difficult kata and more dynamic forms of kumite. Karate as self-defense is one of the most dynamic of all the martial arts. The trained karate student is able to coordinate the mind and body perfectly, thereby allowing one to unleash tremendous physical power at will. It is not the possession of great physical strength that makes a strong karateka; rather it is the ability to coordinate the mind and body. Upon developing this ability, even the smallest person finds that within themselves is the power to deliver a devastating blow to any would-be attacker. The values of karate to modern life are numerous. In our everyday lives we often forget the value of exercise to both our physical and mental health. The practice of karate tones the body, develops coordination, quickens reflexes, and builds stamina. Also, the serious practice of karate develops composure, a clearer thought process, deeper insight into one's mental capabilities, and more self-confidence. In this, karate is not an end, but a means to an end. It is an activity in which advancing age is not a hindrance. Rather, it encourages proficiency and the keen coordination of mind and body.

title: Battle of Food Scientists
date_time: Thursday, March 12 2026 at 4:00 PM CDT to Thursday, March 12 2026 at 8:00 PM CDT
location: Department of Food Science and Technology 1901 N 21st St, Lincoln, Nebraska View Map
description: The 2026 Battle of the Food Scientists is scheduled for March 12, 2026 (4:00–8:00 PM) at the Food Innovation Center. The competition is open to undergraduate and graduate students (Food Science & Technology and other majors) competing in teams of 2–4 (individual registration and team matching available). The 2026 theme, “Fermented Fiesta,” features two rounds: Mystery Basket – Plant-Based Muffin (AI-assisted brainstorming with a revealed mystery ingredient) and the Fermented Flavor Challenge (a dish featuring a fermented ingredient comprising at least 10% of the recipe). Entries will be evaluated by UNL faculty and industry professionals using a rubric that emphasizes creativity, taste, presentation, and effective use of required ingredients. This event aligns with Phi Tau Sigma’s mission by promoting scholarly engagement, experiential learning, industry networking, and chapter visibility through flyers, livestreams, and newsletter/social media features. Chapter members also gain practical experience in event planning, leadership, science communication, and social media outreach. Here’s a quick look at last year’s event: https://mediahub.unl.edu/media/22160

title: Project NANDA Presentation
date_time: Wednesday, March 4 2026 at 2:30 PM CST to Wednesday, March 4 2026 at 3:30 PM CST
location: Kiewit Hall A221 820 N 16th St, Lincoln, NE View Map
description: Project NANDA is building the foundational infrastructure for the Open Agentic Web - a system where trillions of AI agents can collaborate, communicate, and transact across organizational boundaries without bottlenecks or security vulnerabilities. NANDA addresses the core challenge: how can billions or even trillions of AI agents discover each other, verify capabilities, and coordinate tasks without creating bottlenecks or security vulnerabilities. The project develops both the technical infrastructure (index - interop links between all heterogeneous agent registries, protocols, SDKs) and the governance frameworks needed for a responsible, Open Agentic Web . Join us on March 4 to hear from an MIT researcher on Project NANDA!
